title: Berlin in the Rain — Quick Take
items: Berlin gets 100+ rainy days a year — the historic center is genuinely better in the rain. Fewer crowds, dramatic light, and wet cobblestones that reflect the architecture.
Museum Island is your strongest move: 4 active world-class museums plus the free Humboldt Forum atrium, all within a 5-minute walk of Alexanderplatz. (Pergamon stays closed through 2027.)
Mid-walk shelter along the historic route: Marienkirche (free), the Rotes Rathaus foyer, TV Tower, Hackesche Höfe covered courtyards, and the DDR Museum.
Skip the umbrella — Berlin's wind destroys them. A hooded waterproof jacket and a BVG day ticket are what locals actually use.
The 2-hour free walking tour still runs in the rain. Only heavy thunderstorms cancel it.
